article_meta.title: The headquarters of the Euroclear financial institute in Brussels Photograph: Nicolas Maeterlinck/Belga Mag/AFP/Getty Im

reasoning: The summary largely maintains neutral language and attributes charged claims to sources. It echoes terms like “intimidation campaign” but frames them as reports from European intelligence, avoiding the article’s more loaded framing in its own voice.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses neutral verbs (e.g., “report,” “debating,” “seeking”), with limited carryover like “intimidation campaign” clearly attributed.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: Key assertions about targeting and intent are linked to European intelligence; most claims are sourced.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Evaluative terms (e.g., “absolutely critical”) are attributed to Ukrainian experts.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: No amplification beyond source; strong terms are presented with attribution.
custom_reasoning.mudslinging: No personal attacks or character judgments in the model’s voice.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: Intent (“aims to pressure”) derives from cited agencies; minimally inferential.
custom_reasoning.slant: Includes Belgian, Russian, Ukrainian, and Euroclear angles; balanced emphasis.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Minor UK angle present in article is omitted; core perspectives retained.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Limited and attributed; avoids adopting them as its own stance.
custom_reasoning.word_choice: Politically neutral phrasing overall; avoids partisan labels.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Reflects article’s risk-focused content without overstating.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: Uses official and expert sources proportionally; no undue deference.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Noticeably more neutral than source, with consistent attribution.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Covers main sides (EU/Belgium, Russia, Ukraine, Euroclear); omits smaller UK thread.
